# N# top 25 diagnostic golden suite
# Stable terminal rendering from OutputFormatter.DiagnosticsToText.
# Docs URLs are category-qualified where parser/analyzer/linter docs are split.

Diagnostic clusters (25 groups, 25 diagnostics)

       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/cannot-infer.nl --pos 2:5
       example: [analyzer] Cannot infer type
       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a source, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.

       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a source, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.

       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/non-exhaustive-match.nl --pos 3:12
       example: [analyzer] Pattern matching is not exhaustive
       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a source, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.
  [1x] type-mismatch / variable-declaration / risk: medium
       recipe: refactor:signature-or-expression-shape
       root: analyzer/type-mismatch.nl:3:19
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/type-mismatch.nl --pos 3:19
       example: [analyzer] Type mismatch
       next: Compare the expected and actual types at the root example and update the refactor recipe that changed the expression/signature shape.
       next: Prefer fixing the producer expression over adding casts to each cascaded consumer.
  [1x] member-resolution / return-statement / risk: medium
       recipe: members:api-rename-or-extension-import
       root: analyzer/undefined-member.nl:4:17
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-member.nl --pos 4:17
       example: [analyzer] Member 'Lenght' not found on type 'string'
       next: Verify the API/member name for the root receiver before fixing repeated call sites.
       next: Check whether an extension-method import or receiver type conversion was dropped.
  [1x] type-resolution / variable-declaration / risk: medium
       recipe: types:resolve-type-or-import
       root: analyzer/undefined-type.nl:1:12
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-type.nl --pos 1:12
       example: [analyzer] Type 'Usr' not found
       next: Resolve the type/import at the earliest root location before chasing downstream uses.
       next: Check whether the source construct needs full qualification or a project reference.
  [1x] identifier-resolution / return-statement / risk: medium
       recipe: symbols:missing-import-or-qualification
       root: analyzer/undefined-variable.nl:6:12
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-variable.nl --pos 6:12
       example: [analyzer] Variable 'totla' not found
       next: Resolve the first missing identifier by adding the import/qualification or correcting the declaration name.
       next: Rerun diagnostics after the root symbol is resolved; dependent member/type errors may disappear.
  [1x] diagnostic-message-shape / call-or-construction / risk: low
       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/wrong-argument-count.nl --pos 5:5

       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.

       next command: nlc query inspect --file linter/empty-catch.nl --pos 5:7

  ... 15 more clusters omitted; use --json for the full AI-consumable cluster list.

── [NL101] ERROR ──────────── parser/missing-argument.nl:3:17 ──

    3 |     print(name, )
      |                 ^

[parser] Unexpected token ')'

The parser found `)` while it was still looking for an expression argument.

Suggestion: Add the missing expression before `)` or remove the dangling comma.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL101

── [NL102] ERROR ───── parser/missing-parameter-colon.nl:1:12 ──

    1 | func greet(name string) {
      |            ^^^^

[parser] Expected ':' after parameter name

Function parameters use `name: Type`; without the colon, the type name is parsed in the wrong slot.

Suggestion: Write `func greet(name: string) { ... }`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL102

── [NL103] ERROR ─── parser/object-initializer-equals.nl:2:23 ──

    2 |     return new User { Name = "Ada" }
      |                       ^^^^

[parser] Invalid syntax in object initializer

N# object initializers use colon fields; `=` is C# object-initializer syntax.

Suggestion: Use `new User { Name: "Ada" }`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL103

── [NL104] ERROR ──────── parser/missing-closing-brace.nl:5:1 ──


[parser] Unexpected end of file

The file ended before the parser found the closing `}` for the current block.

Suggestion: Add the missing closing brace and re-run `nlc check`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL104

── [NL106] ERROR ────────── parser/missing-match-brace.nl:7:1 ──

    7 | match status {
      | ^^^^^

[parser] Missing closing brace

A block started here but never closed, so later code may be attached to the wrong scope.

Suggestion: Close the block with `}` at the indentation level where the construct began.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL106

── [NL107] ERROR ────────── parser/missing-call-paren.nl:4:22 ──

    4 |     total := add(first, second
      |                      ^

[parser] Missing closing parenthesis

This call opened `(` but did not close it before the line ended.

Suggestion: Add `)` after the final argument: `add(first, second)`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL107

── [NL202] ERROR ───────────── analyzer/type-mismatch.nl:3:19 ──

    3 | let count: int = "five"
      |                   ^^^^^

[analyzer] Type mismatch

This expression produces `string`, but the annotation says `int`.

Suggestion: Parse the string intentionally or change the annotation to `string`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL202

── [NL203] ERROR ─────────────── analyzer/cannot-infer.nl:2:5 ──

    2 | let value = null
      |     ^^^^^

[analyzer] Cannot infer type

`null` by itself does not tell the analyzer which nullable type you want.

Suggestion: Add an explicit type, for example `let value: string? = null`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL203

── [NL301] ERROR ──────── analyzer/undefined-variable.nl:6:12 ──

    6 |     return totla
      |            ^^^^^

[analyzer] Variable 'totla' not found

There is no local, parameter, or member named `totla` in scope.

Suggestion: Did you mean `total`? Fix the spelling or declare the variable before use.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL301

── [NL302] ERROR ──────────── analyzer/undefined-type.nl:1:12 ──

    1 | let user: Usr
      |            ^^^

[analyzer] Type 'Usr' not found

The analyzer cannot resolve `Usr` from this file's declarations or imports.

Suggestion: Import the type, define it, or correct the spelling to `User`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL302

── [NL303] ERROR ────────── analyzer/undefined-member.nl:4:17 ──

    4 |     return name.Lenght
      |                 ^^^^^^

[analyzer] Member 'Lenght' not found on type 'string'

The receiver type is `string`, and `Lenght` is not one of its members.

Suggestion: Did you mean `Length`? Use the exact member name exposed by the type.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL303

── [NL305] ERROR ───────────── analyzer/missing-return.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | func score(ok: bool): int {
      | ^^^^

[analyzer] Not all code paths return a value of type 'int'

This function promises to return `int`, but at least one branch can fall off the end.

Suggestion: Return an `int` on every path, or change the return type to `void` if no value is needed.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL305

── [NL401] ERROR ─────── analyzer/wrong-argument-count.nl:5:5 ──

    5 | send(email)
      |     ^^^^^^^^^

[analyzer] Function 'send' expects 2 arguments but got 1

The call is missing one required argument from the function signature.

Suggestion: Pass the missing value, or update the function signature if it should be optional.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL401

── [NL501] ERROR ────── analyzer/non-exhaustive-match.nl:3:12 ──

    3 |     match color {
      |            ^^^^^

[analyzer] Pattern matching is not exhaustive

The match does not handle every possible value of `Color`.

Suggestion: Add arms for the missing cases or a final `_ => ...` arm when a catch-all is intentional.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL501

── [NL001] ERROR ────────────── linter/unused-variable.nl:2:5 ──

    2 |     temp := 42
      |     ^^^^

[linter] Variable 'temp' is declared but never read

Unused locals are almost always stale code or a missed side effect.

Suggestion: Remove the declaration or prefix it with `_` when the unused value is intentional.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL001

── [NL006] ERROR ───────────── linter/unreachable-code.nl:4:5 ──

    4 |     print "done"
      |     ^^^^^

[linter] Unreachable code detected

Statements after a guaranteed exit cannot run and often hide a control-flow bug.

Suggestion: Move the statement before the exit or delete it.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL006

── [NL010] ERROR ──────────────── linter/unused-import.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | import System.Linq
      | ^^^^^^

[linter] Import 'System.Linq' is never used

Unused imports make dependency intent harder to read and can mask stale code.

Suggestion: Remove the import or use a symbol from it.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL010

── [NL003] WARNING ───── linter/unnecessary-null-check.nl:3:4 ──

    3 | if name != null {
      |    ^^^^

[linter] Unnecessary null check on non-nullable value

The value is already known to be non-nullable, so the condition adds noise without protecting anything.

Suggestion: Delete the null check and keep the useful branch body.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL003

── [NL004] WARNING ──────── linter/async-without-await.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | async func Load(): Task<int> {
      | ^^^^^^^^^^

[linter] Async function has no await

An async function with no await usually does not need the async state machine.

Suggestion: Remove `async` or await the asynchronous operation that should drive this function.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL004

── [NL005] INFO ────────── linter/use-pattern-matching.nl:3:1 ──

    3 | if value is string {
      | ^^

[linter] Use pattern matching

A chain of type or shape checks is easier to audit when expressed as one match.

Suggestion: Rewrite the branch as a `match` when several related cases are being handled.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL005

── [NL011] WARNING ──────────────── linter/empty-catch.nl:5:7 ──

    5 | } catch (ex) {
      |       ^^^^^

[linter] Empty catch block

Swallowing errors silently makes failures hard to debug and can corrupt program state.

Suggestion: Handle the error, log it, or explain the intentional suppression with a comment.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL011

── [NL012] INFO ───────────── linter/unused-parameter.nl:1:15 ──

    1 | func Save(options: SaveOptions) {
      |               ^^^^^^^

[linter] Parameter 'options' is never used

Unused parameters usually mean the call contract drifted from the implementation.

Suggestion: Use the parameter, remove it from the signature, or prefix it with `_` if required by an interface.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL012

── [NL013] INFO ───────── linter/prefer-interpolation.nl:2:12 ──

    2 | message := "Hello, " + name
      |            ^

[linter] Prefer string interpolation

Interpolation keeps formatting intent in one string instead of splitting it across concatenation.

Suggestion: Use `$"Hello, {name}"`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL013

── [NL015] INFO ────────────────── linter/prefer-const.nl:2:5 ──

    2 | let limit := 10
      |     ^^^^^

[linter] Variable 'limit' can be const

Values that never change are clearer when declared as constants.

Suggestion: Change `let` to `const`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL015

── [NL020] WARNING ────────── linter/shadowed-variable.nl:4:9 ──

    4 |         count := item.Count
      |         ^^^^^

[linter] Variable 'count' shadows an outer variable

Shadowing makes reads ambiguous and can cause updates to affect the wrong variable.

Suggestion: Rename the inner variable or reuse the existing one intentionally.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/linter/NL020


Found 17 errors, 4 warnings, 4 info.
